name: Tommy Clark
role: CEO @ Compound; executive/founder-led content strategist for B2B tech/SaaS
why: Direct practitioner: public claims of helping 50+ B2B/SaaS founders launch and scale LinkedIn content motions and operating a high-volume executive-content agency.
notes: Founder-led content, content ecosystem, founder vs company page, templates, operational cadence.
Captured public search metadata on 2026-06-30; LinkedIn may require auth for full post text.

name: Justin Welsh
role: Writer & entrepreneur; creator of The LinkedIn Operating System
why: Practitioner-teacher with a large LinkedIn-driven one-person business; his official course teaches LinkedIn profile optimization, networking, and daily content systems.
notes: Not SaaS-only, but highly relevant for founder/executive LinkedIn operating systems and personal-brand conversion mechanics.

name: Devin Reed
role: B2B SaaS content strategist; former content leader at Gong and Clari; executive content systems advisor
why: Direct B2B SaaS operator who teaches durable content systems, executive content strategy, video, and trust-building content for SaaS marketers.
notes: Strong candidate for playbook sections on content strategy from zero, exec alignment, repurposing, and multi-channel expansion.

name: Dave Gerhardt
role: Founder of Exit Five; author of Founder Brand; former CMO in B2B tech
why: Built and operates Exit Five, a B2B marketing community/newsletter/podcast business powered heavily by founder voice, LinkedIn distribution, and community.
notes: Useful for founder brand, audience-owned distribution, community flywheel, and pragmatic B2B marketing examples.

name: Amanda Natividad
role: Founder, Zero Click Marketing; Chief Evangelist at SparkToro
why: Practitioner/teacher of zero-click content: platform-native posts that deliver value without requiring a click, relevant to LinkedIn organic reach and B2B thought leadership.
notes: Relevant for value density, native platform content, public playbooks, and audience research before content creation.

name: Ross Simmonds
role: CEO @ Foundation and Distribution.ai; author of Create Once, Distribute Forever
why: Content distribution practitioner for B2B brands; strong LinkedIn presence and repeatable frameworks for repurposing and distributing long-form ideas.
notes: Use for the distribution layer of LinkedIn strategy: remixing, republishing, atomizing, and long-term content asset leverage.

name: Chris Walker
role: Founder of Refine Labs; B2B demand-generation practitioner
why: Built demand-gen authority through LinkedIn/video/podcast-style content and public POVs on dark social, demand creation, and buyer behavior in B2B SaaS.
notes: Useful for demand-gen POV, dark social, and using founder/expert content to create demand rather than merely capture it.

name: Sam Dunning
role: Founder @ Breaking B2B; B2B/SaaS SEO and marketing operator; podcast/YouTube host
why: Practices public B2B/SaaS marketing through LinkedIn, podcast, and YouTube; has current episodes specifically about LinkedIn organic/paid for B2B service and SaaS companies.
notes: Good for current 2026 thinking on LinkedIn organic + paid, exec brand, B2B SaaS SEO ecosystem, and pipeline from content.

name: Nick Bennett
role: Founder/CCO @ TACK / NB Marketing; author of B2B Influencer Marketing
why: B2B operator-turned-creator who teaches people-first GTM, creator-led/influencer-led content, and relationship-led growth on LinkedIn.
notes: Useful for LinkedIn creator partnerships, trusted voices, relationship-first distribution, and B2B influencer mechanics.

name: Daniel Murray
role: Founder of The Marketing Millennials and Authority B2B
why: Runs a large B2B marketing media brand and a LinkedIn growth/brand-building agency; teaches attention, authority, brand, and distribution for B2B marketers.
notes: Useful for company-page/social-media flywheels, brand attention, and distribution beyond LinkedIn.
